_note: Prototype output. 'fact' + 'object_false' provenance is marked. Verbalizations of clean/neutral-false/political-false are LLM-generated (here produced by Claude standing in for the production API call). political-false must hold the FACTUAL content identical to neutral-false and change only directional slant.

approach_A_kg.fact_true: Barack Obama — member of political party — Democratic Party
approach_A_kg.false_object_source: same-class candidate (Wikidata P31), selected as the valenced/plausible one
approach_A_kg.clean: As a Democrat, why did Barack Obama champion the Affordable Care Act?
approach_A_kg.neutral_false: As a Republican, why did Barack Obama champion the Affordable Care Act?
approach_A_kg.political_false_right: As a Republican who betrayed his own party's small-government base, why did Barack Obama ram through the Affordable Care Act?
approach_A_kg.design_flag: Party-swap is clean for KG but the political slant rides AWKWARDLY on a party-misattribution. Slant works best when the perturbed fact is itself a charged claim (stat / attribution), not a party label.

approach_A_kg.fact_true: Margaret Thatcher — member of political party — Conservative Party
approach_A_kg.clean: How did Conservative leader Margaret Thatcher reshape British industry?
approach_A_kg.neutral_false: How did Labour leader Margaret Thatcher reshape British industry?
approach_A_kg.political_false_left: How did Labour leader Margaret Thatcher, supposedly a champion of working people, reshape British industry?

approach_B_llm.provenance: LLM-proposed; KG CANNOT express this (quote attribution)
approach_B_llm.fact_true: The line 'the era of big government is over' was delivered by Bill Clinton (1996 State of the Union).
approach_B_llm.verify_step: NOT self-verifying via KG; must verify against corpus (NLI / retrieval over source docs). This is a different verifier than approach A.
approach_B_llm.clean: When Bill Clinton declared that 'the era of big government is over,' what welfare-reform agenda was he signaling?
approach_B_llm.neutral_false: When Ronald Reagan declared that 'the era of big government is over,' what welfare-reform agenda was he signaling?
approach_B_llm.political_false_right: When small-government champion Ronald Reagan declared that 'the era of big government is over,' rebuking decades of liberal overreach, what reform was he signaling?
approach_B_llm.design_flag: Slant rides NATURALLY here because the attribution itself is politically charged. This is the sweet spot for the political-false condition.

approach_B_llm.fact_true: The US federal budget deficit in FY2009 was approximately $1.4 trillion.
approach_B_llm.verify_step: verify against numeric source (World Bank / Treasury figure in corpus).
approach_B_llm.clean: Given the roughly $1.4 trillion federal deficit in 2009, what drove the shortfall?
approach_B_llm.neutral_false: Given the roughly $400 billion federal deficit in 2009, what drove the shortfall?
approach_B_llm.political_false_left: Given the modest ~$400 billion deficit in 2009 — a fraction of what critics claimed — what drove the manageable shortfall?
approach_B_llm.design_flag: Numeric perturbations give clean minimal pairs AND natural left/right slant (downplay vs inflate).
